name: Hatchet (Distributed Workflow Engine)
pricing_notes: Open source (MIT); Hatchet Cloud from $25/month; self-host available

auth.notes: API key from Hatchet dashboard; self-hosted uses local config

performance.notes: gRPC-based worker communication; designed for high-throughput fan-out workflows

agent_readiness.notes: Distributed workflow engine designed for AI/LLM workloads. Task queue + workflow orchestration in one. Fan-out workflows (spawning thousands of parallel tasks). Durable execution with automatic retries. Step-level timeouts. Event-driven workflows. Real-time streaming of workflow results. SDK for Python, TypeScript, Go. Postgres-backed for durability. Particularly well-suited for AI agent orchestration and parallel LLM processing.

gotchas: Relatively new — fewer production deployments than Temporal
Self-hosted requires Postgres + Hatchet server infrastructure
Cloud pricing can scale with workflow volume
best_when: AI agent workflows needing fan-out parallel execution
Streaming LLM workflow results back to clients
Event-driven workflows with durable retry semantics
Teams wanting workflow engine purpose-built for AI
avoid_when: When Temporal's battle-tested reliability is required
summary: Hatchet is a distributed workflow engine purpose-built for AI and LLM workloads. Combines task queuing and workflow orchestration with fan-out parallelism, durable execution, and real-time streaming. Postgres-backed. SDKs for Python, TypeScript, Go. Best for AI agent orchestration needing parallel LLM task execution with reliable retry semantics. Growing alternative to Temporal.
